verses.latin_vulgate: In illo tempore audivit Herodes tetrarcha famam Jesu:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν ἐκείνῳ τῷ καιρῷ ἤκουσεν ἡρῴδης ὁ τετράρχης τὴν ἀκοὴν ἰησοῦ
verses.king_james: At that time Herod the tetrarch heard of the fame of Jesus,

verses.latin_vulgate: et ait pueris suis: Hic est Joannes Baptista: ipse surrexit a mortuis, et ideo virtutes operantur in eo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἶπεν τοῖς παισὶν αὐτοῦ οὗτός ἐστιν ἰωάννης ὁ βαπτιστής αὐτὸς ἠγέρθη ἀπὸ τῶν νεκρῶν καὶ διὰ τοῦτο αἱ δυνάμεις ἐνεργοῦσιν ἐν αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: And said unto his servants, This is John the Baptist; he is risen from the dead; and therefore mighty works do shew forth themselves in him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Herodes enim tenuit Joannem, et alligavit eum: et posuit in carcerem propter Herodiadem uxorem fratris sui.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ γὰρ ἡρῴδης κρατήσας τὸν ἰωάννην ἔδησεν αὐτὸν καὶ ἔθετο ἐν φυλακῇ διὰ ἡρῳδιάδα τὴν γυναῖκα φιλίππου τοῦ ἀδελφοῦ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: For Herod had laid hold on John, and bound him, and put *him* in prison for Herodias' sake, his brother Philip's wife.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicebat enim illi Joannes: Non licet tibi habere eam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔλεγεν γὰρ αὐτῷ ὁ ἰωάννης οὐκ ἔξεστίν σοι ἔχειν αὐτήν
verses.king_james: For John said unto him, It is not lawful for thee to have her.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et volens illum occidere, timuit populum: quia sicut prophetam eum habebant.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ θέλων αὐτὸν ἀποκτεῖναι ἐφοβήθη τὸν ὄχλον ὅτι ὡς προφήτην αὐτὸν εἶχον
verses.king_james: And when he would have put him to death, he feared the multitude, because they counted him as a prophet.

verses.latin_vulgate: Die autem natalis Herodis saltavit filia Herodiadis in medio, et placuit Herodi:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: γενεσίων δὲ ἀγομένων τοῦ ἡρῴδου ὠρχήσατο ἡ θυγάτηρ τῆς ἡρῳδιάδος ἐν τῷ μέσῳ καὶ ἤρεσεν τῷ ἡρῴδῃ
verses.king_james: But when Herod's birthday was kept, the daughter of Herodias danced before them, and pleased Herod.

verses.latin_vulgate: unde cum juramento pollicitus est ei dare quodcumque postulasset ab eo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅθεν μεθ᾽ ὅρκου ὡμολόγησεν αὐτῇ δοῦναι ὃ ἐὰν αἰτήσηται
verses.king_james: Whereupon he promised with an oath to give her whatsoever she would ask.

verses.latin_vulgate: At illa præmonita a matre sua: Da mihi, inquit, hic in disco caput Joannis Baptistæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ δὲ προβιβασθεῖσα ὑπὸ τῆς μητρὸς αὐτῆς δός μοι φησίν ὧδε ἐπὶ πίνακι τὴν κεφαλὴν ἰωάννου τοῦ βαπτιστοῦ
verses.king_james: And she, being before instructed of her mother, said, Give me here John Baptist's head in a charger.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et contristatus est rex: propter juramentum autem, et eos qui pariter recumbebant, jussit dari.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐλυπηθη ὁ βασιλεὺς διὰ δὲ τοὺς ὅρκους καὶ τοὺς συνανακειμένους ἐκέλευσεν δοθῆναι
verses.king_james: And the king was sorry: nevertheless for the oath's sake, and them which sat with him at meat, he commanded *it* to be given *her*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Misitque et decollavit Joannem in carcere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πέμψας ἀπεκεφάλισεν τὸν ἰωάννην ἐν τῇ φυλακῇ
verses.king_james: And he sent, and beheaded John in the prison.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et allatum est caput ejus in disco, et datum est puellæ, et attulit matri suæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἠνέχθη ἡ κεφαλὴ αὐτοῦ ἐπὶ πίνακι καὶ ἐδόθη τῷ κορασίῳ καὶ ἤνεγκεν τῇ μητρὶ αὐτῆς
verses.king_james: And his head was brought in a charger, and given to the damsel: and she brought *it* to her mother.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et accedentes discipuli ejus, tulerunt corpus ejus, et sepelierunt illud: et venientes nuntiaverunt Jesu.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ προσελθόντες οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ ἦραν τὸ σῶμα καὶ ἔθαψαν αὐτό καὶ ἐλθόντες ἀπήγγειλαν τῷ ἰησοῦ
verses.king_james: And his disciples came, and took up the body, and buried it, and went and told Jesus.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod cum audisset Jesus, secessit inde in navicula, in locum desertum seorsum: et cum audissent turbæ, secutæ sunt eum pedestres de civitatibus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀκούσας ὁ ἰησοῦς ἀνεχώρησεν ἐκεῖθεν ἐν πλοίῳ εἰς ἔρημον τόπον κατ᾽ ἰδίαν καὶ ἀκούσαντες οἱ ὄχλοι ἠκολούθησαν αὐτῷ πεζῇ ἀπὸ τῶν πόλεων
verses.king_james: When Jesus heard *of it*, he departed thence by ship into a desert place apart: and when the people had heard *thereof*, they followed him on foot out of the cities.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et exiens vidit turbam multam, et misertus est eis, et curavit languidos eorum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐξελθὼν ὁ ἰησοῦς εἶδεν πολὺν ὄχλον καὶ ἐσπλαγχνίσθη ἐπ᾽ αὐτούς καὶ ἐθεράπευσεν τοὺς ἀῤῥώστους αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: And Jesus went forth, and saw a great multitude, and was moved with compassion toward them, and he healed their sick.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vespere autem facto, accesserunt ad eum discipuli ejus, dicentes: Desertus est locus, et hora jam præteriit: dimitte turbas, ut euntes in castella, emant sibi escas.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὀψίας δὲ γενομένης προσῆλθον αὐτῷ οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ λέγοντες ἕρημός ἐστιν ὁ τόπος καὶ ἡ ὥρα ἤδη παρῆλθεν ἀπόλυσον τοὺς ὄχλους ἵνα ἀπελθόντες εἰς τὰς κώμας ἀγοράσωσιν ἑαυτοῖς βρώματα
verses.king_james: And when it was evening, his disciples came to him, saying, This is a desert place, and the time is now past; send the multitude away, that they may go into the villages, and buy themselves victuals.

verses.latin_vulgate: Jesus autem dixit eis: Non habent necesse ire: date illis vos manducare.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἰησοῦς εἶπεν αὐτοῖς οὐ χρείαν ἔχουσιν ἀπελθεῖν δότε αὐτοῖς ὑμεῖς φαγεῖν
verses.king_james: But Jesus said unto them, They need not depart; give ye them to eat.

verses.latin_vulgate: Responderunt ei: Non habemus hic nisi quinque panes et duos pisces.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ δὲ λέγουσιν αὐτῷ οὐκ ἔχομεν ὧδε εἶ μὴ πέντε ἄρτους καὶ δύο ἰχθύας
verses.king_james: And they say unto him, We have here but five loaves, and two fishes.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui ait eis: Afferte mihi illos huc.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ εἰπεν φέρετέ μοι αὐτοὺς ὧδε
verses.king_james: He said, Bring them hither to me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum jussisset turbam discumbere super fœnum, acceptis quinque panibus et duobus piscibus, aspiciens in cælum benedixit, et fregit, et dedit discipulis panes, discipuli autem turbis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ κελεύσας τοὺς ὄχλους ἀνακλιθῆναι ἐπὶ τοὺς χόρτους καὶ λαβὼν τοὺς πέντε ἄρτους καὶ τοὺς δύο ἰχθύας ἀναβλέψας εἰς τὸν οὐρανὸν εὐλόγησεν καὶ κλάσας ἔδωκεν τοῖς μαθηταῖς τοὺς ἄρτους οἱ δὲ μαθηταὶ τοῖς ὄχλοις
verses.king_james: And he commanded the multitude to sit down on the grass, and took the five loaves, and the two fishes, and looking up to heaven, he blessed, and brake, and gave the loaves to *his* disciples, and the disciples to the multitude.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et manducaverunt omnes, et saturati sunt. Et tulerunt reliquias, duodecim cophinos fragmentorum plenos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἔφαγον πάντες καὶ ἐχορτάσθησαν καὶ ἦραν τὸ περισσεῦον τῶν κλασμάτων δώδεκα κοφίνους πλήρεις
verses.king_james: And they did all eat, and were filled: and they took up of the fragments that remained twelve baskets full.

verses.latin_vulgate: Manducantium autem fuit numerus quinque millia virorum, exceptis mulieribus et parvulis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ δὲ ἐσθίοντες ἦσαν ἄνδρες ὡσεὶ πεντακισχίλιοι χωρὶς γυναικῶν καὶ παιδίων
verses.king_james: And they that had eaten were about five thousand men, beside women and children.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et statim compulit Jesus discipulos ascendere in naviculam, et præcedere eum trans fretum, donec dimitteret turbas.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εὐθέως ἠνάγκασεν ὁ ἰησοῦς τοὺς μαθητὰς αὐτοῦ ἐμβῆναι εἰς τὸ πλοῖον καὶ προάγειν αὐτὸν εἰς τὸ πέραν ἕως οὗ ἀπολύσῃ τοὺς ὄχλους
verses.king_james: And straightway Jesus constrained his disciples to get into a ship, and to go before him unto the other side, while he sent the multitudes away.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dimissa turba, ascendit in montem solus orare. Vespere autem facto solus erat ibi:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀπολύσας τοὺς ὄχλους ἀνέβη εἰς τὸ ὄρος κατ᾽ ἰδίαν προσεύξασθαι ὀψίας δὲ γενομένης μόνος ἦν ἐκεῖ
verses.king_james: And when he had sent the multitudes away, he went up into a mountain apart to pray: and when the evening was come, he was there alone.

verses.latin_vulgate: navicula autem in medio mari jactabatur fluctibus: erat enim contrarius ventus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὸ δὲ πλοῖον ἤδη μέσον τῆς θαλάσσης ἦν βασανιζόμενον ὑπὸ τῶν κυμάτων ἦν γὰρ ἐναντίος ὁ ἄνεμος
verses.king_james: But the ship was now in the midst of the sea, tossed with waves: for the wind was contrary.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quarta enim vigilia noctis, venit ad eos ambulans super mare.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τετάρτῃ δὲ φυλακῇ τῆς νυκτὸς ἀπῆλθεν πρὸς αὐτοὺς ὁ ἰησοῦς περιπατῶν ἐπὶ τῆς θαλάσσης
verses.king_james: And in the fourth watch of the night Jesus went unto them, walking on the sea.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et videntes eum super mare ambulantem, turbati sunt, dicentes: Quia phantasma est. Et præ timore clamaverunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἰδόντες αὐτὸν οἱ μαθηταὶ ἐπὶ τὴν θαλάσσαν περιπατοῦντα ἐταράχθησαν λέγοντες ὅτι φάντασμά ἐστιν καὶ ἀπὸ τοῦ φόβου ἔκραξαν
verses.king_james: And when the disciples saw him walking on the sea, they were troubled, saying, It is a spirit; and they cried out for fear.

verses.latin_vulgate: Statimque Jesus locutus est eis, dicens: Habete fiduciam: ego sum, nolite timere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εὐθὲως δὲ ἐλάλησεν αὐτοῖς ὁ ἰησοῦς λέγων φαρσεῖτε ἐγώ εἰμι μὴ φοβεῖσθε
verses.king_james: But straightway Jesus spake unto them, saying, Be of good cheer; it is I; be not afraid.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondens autem Petrus, dixit: Domine, si tu es, jube me ad te venire super aquas.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀποκριθεὶς δὲ αὐτῷ ὁ πέτρος εἰπεν κύριε εἰ σὺ εἶ κέλευσόν με πρὸς σὲ ἐλθεῖν ἐπὶ τὰ ὕδατα
verses.king_james: And Peter answered him and said, Lord, if it be thou, bid me come unto thee on the water.

verses.latin_vulgate: At ipse ait: Veni. Et descendens Petrus de navicula, ambulabat super aquam ut veniret ad Jesum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ εἶπεν ἐλθέ καὶ καταβὰς ἀπὸ τοῦ πλοίου ὁ πέτρος περιεπάτησεν ἐπὶ τὰ ὕδατα ἐλθεῖν πρὸς τὸν ἰησοῦν
verses.king_james: And he said, Come. And when Peter was come down out of the ship, he walked on the water, to go to Jesus.

verses.latin_vulgate: Videns vero ventum validum, timuit: et cum cœpisset mergi, clamavit dicens: Domine, salvum me fac.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: βλέπων δὲ τὸν ἄνεμον ἰσχυρὸν ἐφοβήθη καὶ ἀρξάμενος καταποντίζεσθαι ἔκραξεν λέγων κύριε σῶσόν με
verses.king_james: But when he saw the wind boisterous, he was afraid; and beginning to sink, he cried, saying, Lord, save me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et continuo Jesus extendens manum, apprehendit eum: et ait illi: Modicæ fidei, quare dubitasti?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εὐθέως δὲ ὁ ἰησοῦς ἐκτείνας τὴν χεῖρα ἐπελάβετο αὐτοῦ καὶ λέγει αὐτῷ ὀλιγόπιστε εἰς τί ἐδίστασας
verses.king_james: And immediately Jesus stretched forth *his* hand, and caught him, and said unto him, O thou of little faith, wherefore didst thou doubt?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum ascendissent in naviculam, cessavit ventus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐμβάντων αὐτῶν εἰς τὸ πλοῖον ἐκόπασεν ὁ ἄνεμος
verses.king_james: And when they were come into the ship, the wind ceased.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui autem in navicula erant, venerunt, et adoraverunt eum, dicentes: Vere Filius Dei es.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ δὲ ἐν τῷ πλοίῳ ἐλθόντες προσεκύνησαν αὐτῷ λέγοντες ἀληθῶς θεοῦ υἱὸς εἶ
verses.king_james: Then they that were in the ship came and worshipped him, saying, Of a truth thou art the Son of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum transfretassent, venerunt in terram Genesar.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ διαπεράσαντες ἦλθον εἰς τὴν γῆν γεννησαρέτ
verses.king_james: And when they were gone over, they came into the land of Gennesaret.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum cognovissent eum viri loci illius, miserunt in universam regionem illam, et obtulerunt ei omnes male habentes:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐπιγνόντες αὐτὸν οἱ ἄνδρες τοῦ τόπου ἐκείνου ἀπέστειλαν εἰς ὅλην τὴν περίχωρον ἐκείνην καὶ προσήνεγκαν αὐτῷ πάντας τοὺς κακῶς ἔχοντας
verses.king_james: And when the men of that place had knowledge of him, they sent out into all that country round about, and brought unto him all that were diseased;

verses.latin_vulgate: et rogabant eum ut vel fimbriam vestimenti ejus tangerent. Et quicumque tetigerunt, salvi facti sunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ παρεκάλουν αὐτὸν ἵνα μόνον ἅψωνται τοῦ κρασπέδου τοῦ ἱματίου αὐτοῦ καὶ ὅσοι ἥψαντο διεσώθησαν
verses.king_james: And besought him that they might only touch the hem of his garment: and as many as touched were made perfectly whole.
